questions.question: Who is Bholi?
questions.answer: Bholi is a simple and neglected girl whose real name is Sulekha.

questions.question: Why was Sulekha called Bholi?
questions.answer: She was called Bholi because she was considered simple and slow.

questions.question: Who was Bholi’s father?
questions.answer: Bholi’s father was Ramlal.

questions.question: What physical problem did Bholi have?
questions.answer: She had pock-marks on her face and a stammering speech.

questions.question: Which illness disfigured Bholi’s face?
questions.answer: Smallpox disfigured Bholi’s face.

questions.question: Why was Bholi neglected at home?
questions.answer: She was neglected because she was considered dull and unattractive.

questions.question: At what age was Bholi sent to school?
questions.answer: Bholi was sent to school at the age of seven.

questions.question: Why was Bholi afraid to go to school?
questions.answer: She did not know what school was and feared being taken away.

questions.question: Who encouraged Bholi at school?
questions.answer: Bholi’s teacher encouraged her.

questions.question: How did the teacher treat Bholi?
questions.answer: The teacher treated Bholi kindly and patiently.

questions.question: What change did education bring in Bholi?
questions.answer: Education gave Bholi confidence and self-respect.

questions.question: Who was Bishamber Nath?
questions.answer: Bishamber Nath was a wealthy grocer.

questions.question: What did Bishamber Nath demand at the wedding?
questions.answer: He demanded dowry.

questions.question: Why did Bholi initially agree to the marriage?
questions.answer: She agreed to protect her parents’ honour.

questions.question: What did Bholi do at the wedding?
questions.answer: She refused to marry Bishamber Nath.

questions.question: Why did Bholi refuse the marriage?
questions.answer: She realised her self-respect and rejected injustice.

questions.question: What quality does Bholi show at the end?
questions.answer: She shows confidence and courage.

questions.question: What promise does Bholi make at the end?
questions.answer: She promises to serve and support her parents.

questions.question: What profession does Bholi choose?
questions.answer: She decides to become a teacher.

questions.question: What social evil is highlighted in the story?
questions.answer: The dowry system is highlighted as a social evil.

questions.question: Who represents progressive thinking in the story?
questions.answer: Bholi’s teacher represents progressive thinking.

questions.question: Why is Bholi called Sulekha at the end?
questions.answer: She gains her true identity and confidence.

questions.question: What is the central theme of ‘Bholi’?
questions.answer: Education and empowerment of women.

questions.question: What lesson does the story give parents?
questions.answer: Parents should educate and respect girl children.

questions.question: Who is the author of ‘Bholi’?
questions.answer: The author of ‘Bholi’ is K.A. Abbas.
